pi_items.source: bahmni_env/evidence_docs/bahmni_seed_manifest.json line 101 - patient created via seed_bahmni.py with realistic demographic data (US Census popular names)

bahmni_env/evidence_docs/bahmni_seed_manifest.json line 104 - identifier assigned during synthetic patient creation. Generated as BAH{i+1:06d} where i is enumeration index in PATIENT_DATA (seed_bahmni.py line 269)

bahmni_env/tasks/end_program_enrollment/setup_task.sh line 35 and verifier.py line 71 - custom synthetic program created via OpenMRS REST API (/program endpoint) during task initialization. Not a standard Bahmni/OpenMRS program.
bahmni_env/tasks/end_program_enrollment/setup_task.sh line 112 - enrollment created with dateEnrolled = (date -u -d '30 days ago'). Offset of -30 days from current date is exact match.

privileged_info_summary: All patient and program data in this task is fully synthetic. The target patient, James Osei, is one of 20 synthetically generated patients created by seed_bahmni.py using realistic demographic distributions from US Census data. The patient has been assigned the identifier BAH000011. The 'Wellness Tracking' program is a custom synthetic program created via OpenMRS API during task setup, not a standard Bahmni/OpenMRS program. The program enrollment was programmatically initialized with an enrollment date set to exactly 30 days prior to the current date.
